oConfigScripts;.

testcase.name: Admin adds a new employee and it reflects in the Express API
testcase.system-out: @addEmployee @admin @smoke @api Scenario: Admin adds a new employee and it reflects in the Express API # src/test/resources/features/AddEmployee.feature:13 Given the application is open at "http://localhost:5173/" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theApplicationIsOpenAt(java.lang.String) When I click the "Admin Portal" card # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iClickTheCard(java.lang.String) Then the admin login form should appear # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theAdminLoginFormShouldAppear() When I enter username "admin" and password "admin123" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iEnterUsernameAndPassword(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) And I click the login button # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iClickTheLoginButton() ✔ Admin dashboard loaded Then I should be redirected to the admin dashboard # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iShouldBeRedirectedToTheAdminDashboard() When I click the Employees nav item # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iClickTheEmployeesNavItem() ✔ Admin Employees page loaded Then the Employee Details page should be displayed # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theEmployeeDetailsPageShouldBeDisplayed() And the employee search box should be visible # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theEmployeeSearchBoxShouldBeVisible() When I click the Add Employee button # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.iClickTheAddEmployeeButton() ✔ Add Employee modal is open Then the Add Employee modal should open # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.theAddEmployeeModalShouldOpen() ✔ Name: Selenium Test User When I fill the employee name with "Selenium Test User" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.iFillTheEmployeeNameWith(java.lang.String) ✔ Email: selenium.test@techems.com And I fill the employee email with "selenium.test@techems.com" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.iFillTheEmployeeEmailWith(java.lang.String) ✔ Role: QA Automation Engineer And I fill the employee role with "QA Automation Engineer" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.iFillTheEmployeeRoleWith(java.lang.String) ✔ Phone: +91 99988 77766 And I fill the employee phone with "+91 99988 77766" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.iFillTheEmployeePhoneWith(java.lang.String) ✔ Location: Chennai, India And I fill the employee location with "Chennai, India" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.iFillTheEmployeeLocationWith(java.lang.String) ✔ Add Employee form submitted And I click the submit Add Employee button # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.iClickTheSubmitAddEmployeeButton() ✔ Add Employee modal closed — form submitted successfully Then the Add Employee modal should close # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.theAddEmployeeModalShouldClose() ✔ 'Selenium Test User' appears in the employee table And the new employee "Selenium Test User" should appear in the employee list # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.theNewEmployeeShouldAppearInTheEmployeeList(java.lang.String) ✔ 'Selenium Test User' confirmed in Express API (db.json) ✔ Postman → GET http://localhost:5000/api/employees → returns this employee ✅ And the employee should be saved to the Express API at "http://localhost:5000/api/employees" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.theEmployeeShouldBeSavedToTheExpressAPIAt(java.lang.String)
testcase.system-err: SLF4J(W): No SLF4J providers were found. SLF4J(W): Defaulting to no-operation (NOP) logger implementation SLF4J(W): See https://www.slf4j.org/codes.html#noProviders for further details. Jul 18, 2026 3:07:48 PM org.openqa.selenium.devtools.CdpVersionFinder findNearestMatch WARNING: Unable to find an exact match for CDP version 150, returning the closest version; found: 149; Please update to a Selenium version that supports CDP version 150

testcase.name: Admin adds a second employee and verifies both are in the list
testcase.system-out: @addEmployee @admin @regression Scenario: Admin adds a second employee and verifies both are in the list # src/test/resources/features/AddEmployee.feature:37 Given the application is open at "http://localhost:5173/" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theApplicationIsOpenAt(java.lang.String) When I click the "Admin Portal" card # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iClickTheCard(java.lang.String) Then the admin login form should appear # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theAdminLoginFormShouldAppear() When I enter username "admin" and password "admin123" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iEnterUsernameAndPassword(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) And I click the login button # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iClickTheLoginButton() ✔ Admin dashboard loaded Then I should be redirected to the admin dashboard # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iShouldBeRedirectedToTheAdminDashboard() When I click the Employees nav item # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iClickTheEmployeesNavItem() ✔ Admin Employees page loaded Then the Employee Details page should be displayed # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theEmployeeDetailsPageShouldBeDisplayed() When I click the Add Employee button # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.iClickTheAddEmployeeButton() ✔ Add Employee modal is open Then the Add Employee modal should open # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.theAddEmployeeModalShouldOpen() ✔ Name: Integration Test User When I fill the employee name with "Integration Test User" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.iFillTheEmployeeNameWith(java.lang.String) ✔ Email: integration.test@techems.com And I fill the employee email with "integration.test@techems.com" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.iFillTheEmployeeEmailWith(java.lang.String) ✔ Role: Backend Developer And I fill the employee role with "Backend Developer" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.iFillTheEmployeeRoleWith(java.lang.String) ✔ Phone: +91 88877 66655 And I fill the employee phone with "+91 88877 66655" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.iFillTheEmployeePhoneWith(java.lang.String) ✔ Location: Hyderabad, India And I fill the employee location with "Hyderabad, India" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.iFillTheEmployeeLocationWith(java.lang.String) ✔ Add Employee form submitted And I click the submit Add Employee button # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.iClickTheSubmitAddEmployeeButton() ✔ Add Employee modal closed — form submitted successfully Then the Add Employee modal should close # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.theAddEmployeeModalShouldClose() ✔ 'Integration Test User' appears in the employee table And the new employee "Integration Test User" should appear in the employee list # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.AddEmployeeStepDefs.theNewEmployeeShouldAppearInTheEmployeeList(java.lang.String)
testcase.system-err: Jul 18, 2026 3:08:03 PM org.openqa.selenium.devtools.CdpVersionFinder findNearestMatch WARNING: Unable to find an exact match for CDP version 150, returning the closest version; found: 149; Please update to a Selenium version that supports CDP version 150

testcase.name: Admin logs in and views Employee Details
testcase.classname: Employee Management System - Full Navigation Flow
testcase.system-out: @login @admin @smoke Scenario: Admin logs in and views Employee Details # src/test/resources/features/Login.feature:13 Given the application is open at "http://localhost:5173/" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theApplicationIsOpenAt(java.lang.String) When I click the "Admin Portal" card # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iClickTheCard(java.lang.String) Then the admin login form should appear # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theAdminLoginFormShouldAppear() When I enter username "admin" and password "admin123" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iEnterUsernameAndPassword(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) And I click the login button # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iClickTheLoginButton() ✔ Admin dashboard loaded Then I should be redirected to the admin dashboard # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iShouldBeRedirectedToTheAdminDashboard() When I click the Employees nav item # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iClickTheEmployeesNavItem() ✔ Admin Employees page loaded Then the Employee Details page should be displayed # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theEmployeeDetailsPageShouldBeDisplayed() And the employee search box should be visible # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theEmployeeSearchBoxShouldBeVisible() When I click view on the first employee # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iClickViewOnTheFirstEmployee() ✔ Employee detail modal opened Then the employee detail modal should open # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theEmployeeDetailModalShouldOpen()
testcase.system-err: Jul 18, 2026 3:08:15 PM org.openqa.selenium.devtools.CdpVersionFinder findNearestMatch WARNING: Unable to find an exact match for CDP version 150, returning the closest version; found: 149; Please update to a Selenium version that supports CDP version 150

testcase.name: Employee logs in and views their Dashboard
testcase.classname: Employee Management System - Full Navigation Flow
testcase.system-out: @login @employee @smoke Scenario: Employee logs in and views their Dashboard # src/test/resources/features/Login.feature:28 Given the application is open at "http://localhost:5173/" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theApplicationIsOpenAt(java.lang.String) When I click the "Employee Portal" card # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iClickTheCard(java.lang.String) Then the employee login form should appear # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theEmployeeLoginFormShouldAppear() When I enter username "asbar" and password "emp123" # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iEnterUsernameAndPassword(java.lang.String,java.lang.String) And I click the login button # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iClickTheLoginButton() ✔ Employee dashboard loaded Then I should be redirected to the employee dashboard # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iShouldBeRedirectedToTheEmployeeDashboard() ✔ Attendance card visible And the employee attendance card should be visible # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theEmployeeAttendanceCardShouldBeVisible() ✔ Salary card visible And the employee salary card should be visible # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theEmployeeSalaryCardShouldBeVisible() When I click the My Profile nav item # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.iClickTheMyProfileNavItem() ✔ My Profile page loaded Then the profile page should be displayed # com.employee.automation.stepdefs.LoginStepDefs.theProfilePageShouldBeDisplayed()
testcase.system-err: Jul 18, 2026 3:08:21 PM org.openqa.selenium.devtools.CdpVersionFinder findNearestMatch WARNING: Unable to find an exact match for CDP version 150, returning the closest version; found: 149; Please update to a Selenium version that supports CDP version 150
